examples.name: Minimal base harness
examples.pattern: Kernel smoke-test scaffold
examples.scenario: A new package scaffold needs a single doctor command before any domain agents are added.
workflow_steps: Initialize the harness workspace.
Load the kernel and host adapter.
Check MCP wiring, memory backend, and host adapter health.
Return one PASS/FAIL report with remediation hints.
policy_gates: No domain action is allowed from the minimal scaffold.

examples.name: DevOps incident response
examples.pattern: Alert -> runbook -> gated action -> escalation -> postmortem
examples.scenario: Checkout latency crosses the paging threshold while database saturation is suspected.
workflow_steps: Classify alert severity and impacted service.
Find the closest runbook and propose bounded diagnostics.
Gate shell, network, and kubectl actions before execution.
Escalate when severity or uncertainty crosses policy.
Draft timeline, root cause, contributing factors, and follow-ups.
policy_gates: Production mutations require explicit human approval.
Escalation is mandatory for high severity or unclear blast radius.

input.alert: p95 checkout latency above 2500ms for 10 minutes

examples.name: Senior engineering pod
examples.pattern: Architect -> implementer -> reviewer -> test-writer
examples.scenario: A repository issue asks for deterministic retry behavior around a flaky API client.
workflow_steps: Convert the issue into a minimal file-level implementation plan.
Write or update the focused failing test.
Implement the smallest local-style patch.
Review the diff for correctness, security, reuse, and overengineering.
Run validation gates and summarize residual risk.
policy_gates: No implementation starts before a concrete plan exists.
No merge recommendation is allowed before tests and review.

input.issue: Retry transient 503 responses up to three times with jitter.

examples.name: Evidence dossier
examples.pattern: Decompose -> search -> grade -> synthesize -> fact-check -> cite
examples.scenario: A product team asks whether passkeys reduce account takeover risk for consumer apps.
workflow_steps: Break the research question into independent sub-questions.
Collect primary and secondary sources for each sub-question.
Grade sources for authority, recency, bias, and relevance.
Synthesize only claims backed by accepted evidence.
Attack each claim and normalize citations.
policy_gates: Unsupported claims must be removed or marked as assumptions.

input.question: Do passkeys reduce account takeover risk for consumer apps?

examples.name: Paper trading decision
examples.pattern: Watch -> signal -> risk gate -> paper execution -> attribution
examples.scenario: A momentum signal appears in a liquid ETF, but portfolio drawdown is near the weekly limit.
workflow_steps: Summarize market state and relevant conditions.
Propose signal, confidence, horizon, and invalidation criteria.
Check exposure, drawdown, liquidity, concentration, and circuit breakers.
Route only approved orders to paper execution.
Attribute outcome to thesis, timing, risk, or noise.
policy_gates: Risk Checker is non-bypassable.
Executor runs paper-by-default unless live trading is explicitly approved.

examples.name: Customer support resolution
examples.pattern: Triage -> KB retrieval -> grounded response -> escalation
examples.scenario: A frustrated enterprise customer reports that SSO stopped working after a certificate rotation.
workflow_steps: Classify intent, urgency, customer tier, and sentiment.
Retrieve cited policy and product answers from the KB.
Draft a grounded customer-facing response.
Escalate when confidence, policy coverage, tone, or authority requires it.
Store distilled account and ticket learning.
policy_gates: The responder abstains instead of inventing product or policy details.

input.ticket: SSO login fails after IdP certificate rotation.

examples.name: Draft-only contract review
examples.pattern: Redline -> citation check -> risk rating -> human lawyer
examples.scenario: A vendor MSA includes broad indemnity and unilateral renewal language that conflicts with the playbook.
workflow_steps: Compare clauses against the contract playbook.
Propose clause-level draft redlines.
Verify every cited authority or policy reference.
Score residual legal and commercial risk per clause.
Route draft redline and memo for licensed human review.
policy_gates: Output is draft-only and is never presented as legal advice.
Licensed human review is required before external use.

examples.name: KPI-grounded strategy
examples.pattern: Analyze -> choose bet -> operationalize
examples.scenario: A business unit must choose one quarterly growth bet after pipeline conversion slowed.
workflow_steps: Turn current metrics into findings.
Choose one strategic bet and state trade-offs.
Translate the bet into owners, actions, milestones, and KPIs.
Review feasibility and metric alignment.
policy_gates: Strategic recommendations must tie directly to metrics.

examples.name: CRM lifecycle management
examples.pattern: Qualify -> manage account -> watch churn
examples.scenario: A mid-market account expands usage but has three unresolved support tickets before renewal.
workflow_steps: Score fit, urgency, potential value, and routing.
Choose the next relationship play.
Monitor churn signals and explain early risk.
Update lifecycle memory with distilled state.
policy_gates: CRM mutation is suggestion-only unless explicitly permitted.

examples.name: Campaign strategy
examples.pattern: Audience -> message -> content -> SEO/analytics loop
examples.scenario: A B2B SaaS team wants a launch campaign for a new compliance dashboard.
workflow_steps: Define audience, positioning, channel, and target metric.
Draft channel-specific assets.
Ground content in search demand and analytics.
Revise campaign brief before human review and publishing.
Feed measured results back into memory.
policy_gates: Traffic, SEO, and competitor claims require analytics or cited evidence.

input.icp: security operations leaders at mid-market SaaS companies

examples.name: Media optimization
examples.pattern: Media plan -> creative -> performance readout -> budget reallocation
examples.scenario: A paid acquisition team has a fixed budget and must rebalance underperforming channels weekly.
workflow_steps: Allocate spend across channels based on objective and audience.
Create format-specific creative variants.
Read performance metrics against KPIs and stop-loss thresholds.
Recommend budget reallocation and flag weak creative or channel fit.
policy_gates: Creative ideation cannot control spend reallocation without performance evidence.

examples.name: ML lifecycle
examples.pattern: Data -> train -> evaluate -> deploy behind guardrails
examples.scenario: An ML team wants to ship a classifier for support-ticket routing only if it beats the baseline across slices.
workflow_steps: Build a dataset card with provenance and limits.
Run reproducible training or fine-tuning.
Compare against baseline, subgroup slices, and failure cases.
Deploy only if evaluation gates pass.
Monitor post-deploy drift and regressions.
policy_gates: Evaluator is independent; training metrics alone cannot drive deployment.

input.objective: route support tickets by product area

examples.name: Controlled swarm
examples.pattern: Orchestrator -> planner -> workers -> critic
examples.scenario: A harness must evaluate three independent integration options without exceeding a fixed budget.
workflow_steps: Set objective, budget, max depth, and stopping condition.
Decompose work into dependency-aware tasks.
Run bounded worker tasks.
Review outputs through a critic before landing them.
Decide whether to continue, retry, or stop and store distilled lessons.
policy_gates: No run starts without budget, max depth, stopping condition, and critic gate.

input.stopping_condition: one recommended provider with unresolved risks listed

examples.name: Retrieval review
examples.pattern: Index -> retrieve -> answer -> source review
examples.scenario: A documentation assistant answers a policy question from a newly indexed handbook corpus.
workflow_steps: Chunk, embed, and store source documents.
Retrieve candidate passages with citations.
Draft an answer only from retrieved evidence.
Grade answer fidelity against sources.
Reject or retry weak and unsupported answers.
policy_gates: Retrieval confidence and answer faithfulness are assessed separately.

input.question: What is the parental leave policy for contractors?

examples.name: Safe wellness coordination
examples.pattern: Intake -> red-flag triage -> resource coordination
examples.scenario: A user describes stress, poor sleep, and a concerning symptom while asking for next steps.
workflow_steps: Collect structured wellness information without diagnosing.
Detect red flags and route to appropriate resource type.
Coordinate logistics, reminders, and non-clinical follow-up.
Redirect clinical decisions to a clinician.
policy_gates: No diagnosis, treatment plan, or emergency minimization.
Clinical content is routed to professional care.

examples.name: Playtest-to-design
examples.pattern: Read playtest -> critique balance -> model economy -> preserve narrative
examples.scenario: A playtest shows players abandoning the second dungeon while economy telemetry shows gold inflation.
workflow_steps: Extract signal from telemetry, feedback, and design docs.
Identify mechanic imbalance and propose changes.
Check loops, sinks, inflation, and progression.
Review lore and story consistency.
Write a build-to-build design diff.
policy_gates: Player anecdotes are separated from telemetry-backed findings.

examples.name: B2B pipeline
examples.pattern: Prospect -> qualify -> demo -> close honestly
examples.scenario: A sales team evaluates whether a healthcare prospect is ready for a technical demo.
workflow_steps: Research account context and buying signals.
Score qualification and list missing facts.
Create a personalized demo path.
Handle objections within negotiation boundaries.
Identify pipeline bottleneck and next step.
policy_gates: No-stretch policy: never exaggerate product capabilities.

examples.name: Mastery learning
examples.pattern: Tutor -> explain -> quiz -> grade -> update mastery
examples.scenario: A learner knows linear equations but struggles to apply slope-intercept form in word problems.
workflow_steps: Choose next concept from the mastery map.
Explain at the right depth for the learner profile.
Generate calibrated questions.
Grade answers against a rubric.
Update mastery memory and recommend next focus.
policy_gates: The tutor abstains instead of faking certainty.

examples.name: Repo stewardship
examples.pattern: Triage diff -> benchmark -> release check -> security scan
examples.scenario: A release candidate changes CLI wiring, dependencies, and generated template files.
workflow_steps: Identify changed areas, risks, and review order.
Run performance and regression gates.
Flag risky MCP grants, leaked secrets, and dangerous diffs.
Draft release body and validate readiness gates.
Produce release or no-release recommendation.
policy_gates: The recommendation is conservative and does not overstate readiness.

examples.name: Self-evolution
examples.pattern: Hypothesize -> sandbox experiment -> record -> maybe federate
examples.scenario: A harness proposes changing retrieval weights after repeated low-confidence answers.
workflow_steps: Propose a falsifiable improvement with a declared metric.
Run the experiment only in a sandbox.
Write success or failure to a witness-signed evolution log.
Preserve failed experiments as learning.
Federate only vetted, non-sensitive improvements.
policy_gates: Self-evolution is opt-in and sandboxed.
Production self-mutation and proxy-metric optimization require review.

input.hypothesis: hybrid retrieval weight should increase sparse score from 0.35 to 0.45

examples.name: Algorithmic control plane
examples.pattern: Model proposes; harness decides; algorithms verify
examples.scenario: A user asks the harness to update dependencies, but confidence, risk, cost, and verification gates must pass first.
workflow_steps: Classify intent, risk, expected cost, and verification strategy.
Create a plan and route each step to the right worker or model.
Broker tool access under policy.
Verify outputs independently before applying results.
Write receipt and memory update after verified completion.
policy_gates: Execution is blocked unless confidence, risk, cost, and verification gates pass.

input.request: upgrade minor dependencies and open a patch

examples.name: Repo genome to harness plan
examples.pattern: Static repo genome -> recommended harness plan
examples.scenario: A meta-harness analyzes a repository without executing code and recommends the safest generated harness shape.
workflow_steps: Scan repository structure, languages, tests, and release surfaces without execution.
Score test confidence, MCP risk, and publish readiness.
Recommend agent topology, tool surface, memory namespaces, and approvals.
Emit a harness plan with constraints and required human gates.
policy_gates: Repo analysis does not execute repository code.

examples.name: Planner executor verifier
examples.pattern: Planner -> executor -> verifier
examples.scenario: A coding harness must fix a bug while keeping the approver independent from the patch author.
workflow_steps: Planner decomposes the issue into small steps and acceptance checks.
Executor edits files or drafts the patch within the plan.
Verifier runs tests and reviews the diff independently.
Verifier rejects, requests retry, or approves with residual risk.
policy_gates: The same agent cannot create and approve the output.

input.bug: cache returns stale data after tenant switch

examples.name: Default-deny tool broker
examples.pattern: Governed tool surface with explicit grants
examples.scenario: An agent requests shell, network, and file-write access while diagnosing a failing build.
workflow_steps: Declare requested tool, risk, timeout, and call budget.
Evaluate request against default-deny policy.
Require approval for dangerous or write-capable operations.
Execute only approved calls and record the audit trail.
policy_gates: Network, shell, and file writes are denied unless explicitly granted.

examples.name: Test-first coding pod
examples.pattern: Understand issue -> failing test -> patch -> tests -> diff review
examples.scenario: A repo-maintenance agent fixes a parsing regression by proving the failure before editing production code.
workflow_steps: Understand the issue and target behavior.
Write the failing test that reproduces the issue.
Patch only the code needed to pass the test.
Run relevant and full validation gates.
Review the diff and summarize release notes.
policy_gates: No production code is edited before a failing test exists.

input.issue: markdown parser drops escaped dots in numbered headings

examples.name: Evidence-first research
examples.pattern: Retriever -> source ranker -> claim extractor -> contradiction finder -> synthesizer -> citation verifier
examples.scenario: A research assistant must answer a technical market question with primary-source support.
workflow_steps: Retrieve candidate sources for each sub-question.
Rank each source for authority, recency, balance, and relevance.
Extract claims and identify contradictions.
Synthesize only supported claims.
Verify every citation against the claim it supports.
policy_gates: The synthesizer cannot use claims that failed citation verification.

input.question: Which open model licenses permit commercial fine-tuning?

examples.name: Memory distillation
examples.pattern: Retrieve -> judge -> distill -> consolidate
examples.scenario: A coding harness stores only durable lessons from a failed dependency upgrade, not the full noisy trace.
workflow_steps: Retrieve relevant prior task traces and lessons.
Judge whether the current trajectory produced useful learning.
Distill durable patterns, failures, and verified decisions.
Consolidate into namespace-specific memory with decay metadata.
policy_gates: Do not store raw sensitive task history when a distilled lesson is sufficient.

input.task_trace: dependency upgrade failed due peer dependency conflict

examples.name: Domain-specific judge and distill
examples.pattern: Domain judge -> domain distiller -> reusable lesson
examples.scenario: Different verticals evaluate success with their own domain-specific outcome criteria.
workflow_steps: Apply domain-specific success criteria to the completed run.
Separate domain verdict from generic task completion.
Distill a reusable lesson for that vertical.
Store the lesson in the correct namespace with evidence.
policy_gates: Generic success metrics cannot replace domain-specific judgment.

input.run_result: answer passed grammar checks but had weak source diversity

examples.name: Cost-aware model cascade
examples.pattern: Cheap draft -> verify -> stronger retry only if needed -> record cost and quality
examples.scenario: A CI triage agent drafts issue labels cheaply and escalates only uncertain cases to a stronger model.
workflow_steps: Attempt the task with the cheapest suitable model.
Verify confidence, quality, and safety.
Escalate to a stronger model only when verification fails or uncertainty is high.
Record cost, latency, and quality outcome.
policy_gates: Escalation is evidence-driven, not automatic.

examples.name: Receipt-led governance
examples.pattern: Every meaningful action writes a tamper-evident receipt
examples.scenario: A harness performs a dependency audit and records all material inputs, outputs, tools, and verdicts.
workflow_steps: Hash inputs and planned outputs before action.
Record agent, model, tool calls, cost, latency, risk, and verdict.
Hash-chain the receipt to the previous receipt.
Expose the receipt for audit and replay.
policy_gates: Material actions without receipts are treated as incomplete.

examples.name: CI agent
examples.pattern: Trigger -> bounded task -> GitHub API tools -> PR/comment/status output
examples.scenario: A GitHub Action reviews a pull request and posts a bounded genome report without arbitrary shell access.
workflow_steps: Start from a CI trigger with declared permissions.
Run a bounded, non-interactive task.
Use GitHub API tools instead of arbitrary shell by default.
Post PR comment, status, or release note output.
policy_gates: No arbitrary shell or broad token permissions in CI by default.

examples.name: Human approval gate
examples.pattern: Draft -> explain risk -> request approval -> execute approved action -> record receipt
examples.scenario: An agent wants to publish a package release after tests pass but before human release approval.
workflow_steps: Draft the proposed action and explain risk.
Request approval for the exact action.
Execute only the approved operation.
Record approval, execution, and receipt.
policy_gates: Dangerous actions execute only after explicit approval of the exact action.

examples.name: Opt-in self-evolution
examples.pattern: Measure baseline -> small exploration -> evaluate -> adopt if better -> rollback on regression
examples.scenario: A harness tests a routing-weight change against historical tasks before adopting it.
workflow_steps: Measure current baseline on declared metrics.
Run a small opt-in exploration in sandbox.
Evaluate against baseline and regression thresholds.
Adopt only if better, otherwise preserve failure learning.
Write rollback path and audit log.
policy_gates: Adaptive tuning is opt-in and rollback-ready.

examples.name: Private/shared federation
examples.pattern: Local confidential work -> distilled non-sensitive pattern -> signed sharing -> peer validation
examples.scenario: An enterprise team shares a generic failure pattern learned from internal incidents without exposing customer data.
workflow_steps: Distill non-sensitive pattern from confidential local work.
Filter user data, secrets, and task history.
Sign the reusable pattern and publish to an allowed namespace.
Validate peer patterns before import.
policy_gates: Federation shares patterns, not user data.

input.local_lesson: rollback runbooks need precomputed owner mapping

examples.name: STORM article synthesis
examples.pattern: Discover perspectives -> ask multi-perspective questions -> retrieve and ground -> outline -> write sections -> audit citations
examples.scenario: A documentation team must produce a comprehensive, neutral overview article on an unfamiliar topic, grounded entirely in cited sources rather than model memory.
workflow_steps: Discover diverse perspectives by surveying related topics and articles.
Drive multi-perspective question asking through simulated expert conversations.
Convert questions into queries, retrieve trusted sources, and answer with citations.
Generate and refine a hierarchical outline from internal knowledge and collected answers.
Write each section grounded only in collected references with inline citations.
Assemble the lead section, remove duplication, and verify every citation.
policy_gates: Article claims must be grounded in retrieved, cited sources, not in model memory.
Untrusted or unverifiable sources are excluded before they ground any section.

reference.title: Assisting in Writing Wikipedia-like Articles From Scratch with Large Language Models
